story.iWant: custom Keycloak Docker image built for ppc64le architecture
story.soThat: EAF supports all required processor architectures (amd64, arm64, ppc64le)
story.tasks:
### Tasks/Subtasks
#### Task 1: Create Dockerfile.ppc64le - [ ] Create docker/keycloak/Dockerfile.ppc64le with UBI9 base image - [ ] Implement multi-stage build (UBI9 → Maven build → Runtime image) - [ ] Configure Keycloak 26.4.2 build from source - [ ] Optimize image size (remove build artifacts in final stage)
#### Task 2: Create build automation script - [ ] Create scripts/build-keycloak-ppc64le.sh - [ ] Add command-line parameters (version, registry, tag) - [ ] Document script usage and prerequisites - [ ] Make script executable and add error handling
#### Task 3: Test image on ppc64le - [ ] Test build on QEMU ppc64le emulation OR real hardware if available - [ ] Verify Keycloak starts successfully - [ ] Test OIDC endpoints (/.well-known/openid-configuration) - [ ] Test realm import functionality
#### Task 4: Push image to container registry - [ ] Build and tag image as keycloak:26.4.2-ppc64le - [ ] Configure registry authentication - [ ] Push image to container registry - [ ] Verify image is pullable from registry
#### Task 5: Create docker-compose variant - [ ] Create docker-compose.ppc64le.yml - [ ] Configure to use custom ppc64le image - [ ] Test complete stack startup (PostgreSQL + Keycloak + Redis) - [ ] Verify network connectivity between services
#### Task 6: Document build process and maintenance - [ ] Create docs/reference/multi-arch-builds.md - [ ] Document UBI9 base image selection rationale - [ ] Document Maven build process - [ ] Document quarterly rebuild schedule (align with Keycloak releases) - [ ] Document troubleshooting steps (QEMU, registry issues)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. ✅ docker/keycloak/Dockerfile.ppc64le created (UBI9-based build) 2. ✅ Multi-stage build: UBI9 → Maven build → Runtime image 3. ✅ Build script: scripts/build-keycloak-ppc64le.sh automates image creation 4. ✅ Image tested on ppc64le emulation (QEMU) or real hardware if available 5. ✅ Image pushed to container registry with tag: keycloak:26.4.2-ppc64le 6. ✅ docker-compose.ppc64le.yml variant uses custom image 7. ✅ Build process documented in docs/reference/multi-arch-builds.md 8. ✅ Quarterly rebuild schedule documented (align with Keycloak releases)

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: The system shall provide IdP abstraction layer supporting multiple providers (Keycloak, Auth0, Okta) with 10-layer JWT validation. Multi-architecture support (amd64, arm64, ppc64le) ensures deployment flexibility.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Keycloak 26.4.2 selected as current stable version. Quarterly version review recommended to align with Keycloak ppc64le rebuild schedule. Multi-architecture build support (amd64/arm64/ppc64le) documented in Section 10.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Multi-Architecture: amd64, arm64, ppc64le required for framework reusability. Custom Keycloak ppc64le build (UBI9-based) represents €4.4K investment for future-proofing. No official ppc64le images available from Keycloak project.

artifact.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: Custom ppc64le Keycloak Docker image (UBI9-based), multi-stage build (UBI9 → Maven → Runtime), build automation script (scripts/build-keycloak-ppc64le.sh), quarterly rebuild schedule (align with Keycloak releases). Prerequisites: Story 3.1 (Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server Foundation).

artifact.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: 8 acceptance criteria covering Dockerfile creation, multi-stage build, automation script, ppc64le testing, registry push, docker-compose variant, documentation, and quarterly rebuild schedule. Testing includes QEMU ppc64le emulation or real hardware validation.

artifact.title: Keycloak Test Realm Configuration
artifact.snippet: Pre-configured Keycloak realm with test users (admin@eaf.com, viewer@eaf.com), roles (WIDGET_ADMIN, WIDGET_VIEWER, ADMIN), client configuration (eaf-api), and tenant_id claim mapper. This configuration should be compatible with ppc64le image.

artifact.reason: Existing documentation for Keycloak Docker setup - reference for ppc64le variant structure
Test realm configuration to be imported in ppc64le image - validates OIDC functionality

docker.purpose: Base image for UBI9-based Keycloak build

tools.purpose: ppc64le emulation for testing on amd64/arm64 hosts

story-context.constraints: - **UBI9 Base Image Required**: Red Hat Universal Base Image 9 provides official ppc64le support and long-term maintenance. - **Multi-Stage Build**: Separate build stage (with Maven) from runtime stage to minimize final image size. - **Keycloak Version Lock**: Must build Keycloak 26.4.2 specifically to match version used in amd64/arm64 deployments. - **Quarterly Rebuild**: Image must be rebuilt quarterly to incorporate security patches and align with Keycloak release cycle. - **No Official ppc64le Support**: Keycloak project does not provide official ppc64le images - custom build is required. - **Registry Compatibility**: Image must be pushable to standard container registries (Docker Hub, GitHub Container Registry, etc.). - **QEMU Testing Acceptable**: Testing on QEMU ppc64le emulation is acceptable if real ppc64le hardware is unavailable. - **Compatibility with Existing Setup**: ppc64le image must import realm-export.json and work with existing EAF Keycloak configuration.

interface.name: Keycloak OIDC Endpoints
interface.note: ppc64le image must expose same OIDC endpoints as amd64/arm64 images

interface.name: Docker Compose Services
interface.signature: services: keycloak: image: keycloak:26.4.2-ppc64le ports: - "8080:8080" environment: - KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin - KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin volumes: - ./realm-export.json:/opt/keycloak/data/import/realm-export.json command: start-dev --import-realm
interface.note: Must maintain compatibility with existing docker-compose.yml structure

interface.name: Build Script Interface
interface.signature: #!/bin/bash # Usage: ./scripts/build-keycloak-ppc64le.sh [OPTIONS] # Options: # --version VERSION Keycloak version to build (default: 26.4.2) # --registry REGISTRY Container registry URL (default: docker.io) # --tag TAG Image tag (default: keycloak:VERSION-ppc64le) # --push Push image to registry after build
interface.note: Script must be idempotent and support dry-run mode

tests.standards:
Testing for Story 3.11 focuses on build validation and functional verification rather than unit/integration tests. Use **manual testing** and **build verification** to ensure the ppc64le image works correctly.
**Build Verification:** - Dockerfile builds successfully without errors - Multi-stage build produces optimized image size (<500MB final image) - Image runs on QEMU ppc64le emulation (qemu-system-ppc64 or Docker buildx)
**Functional Testing:** - Keycloak server starts successfully (health check: curl http://localhost:8080/health) - Admin console accessible (http://localhost:8080/admin) - OIDC discovery endpoint returns valid configuration (/.well-known/openid-configuration) - Realm import works (realm-export.json successfully imported)
**Integration Testing:** - Docker Compose stack starts with ppc64le image - PostgreSQL connection works - JWT token generation functional (POST /realms/eaf/protocol/openid-connect/token)
**Documentation Testing:** - Build process documented clearly enough for another developer to reproduce - Troubleshooting section covers common QEMU and registry issues
tests.locations: - Manual testing: QEMU ppc64le environment or real ppc64le hardware - Build verification: docker build command output - Functional testing: curl commands against Keycloak endpoints - Integration testing: docker-compose up with ppc64le variant

ideas.idea: **Build Test:** Verify Dockerfile.ppc64le builds successfully - Test: `docker buildx build --platform linux/ppc64le -f docker/keycloak/Dockerfile.ppc64le -t keycloak:26.4.2-ppc64le .` - Success: Image builds without errors - Verify: Multi-stage build (check build logs for Maven build stage and runtime stage)

**Automation Test:** Verify build-keycloak-ppc64le.sh script works - Test: `./scripts/build-keycloak-ppc64le.sh --version 26.4.2 --registry localhost:5000 --tag test-ppc64le` - Success: Script executes without errors and produces tagged image - Verify: Script supports --help flag and shows usage

**QEMU Test:** Verify image runs on ppc64le emulation - Test: `docker run --platform linux/ppc64le -it keycloak:26.4.2-ppc64le start-dev` - Success: Keycloak starts and listens on port 8080 - Verify: Health check endpoint returns 200 OK

**Registry Test:** Verify image can be pushed and pulled - Test: `docker push localhost:5000/keycloak:26.4.2-ppc64le && docker pull localhost:5000/keycloak:26.4.2-ppc64le` - Success: Push and pull complete without errors - Verify: Image layers match between local and registry

**Docker Compose Test:** Verify ppc64le variant works - Test: `docker-compose -f docker-compose.ppc64le.yml up -d` - Success: All services start successfully - Verify: Keycloak connects to PostgreSQL and realm import succeeds

**Documentation Test:** Verify multi-arch-builds.md is comprehensive - Test: Follow documentation step-by-step to build image - Success: Another developer can reproduce build without assistance - Verify: Quarterly rebuild schedule is clearly documented with Keycloak release calendar
